Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-20 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Long-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns from 1 month to 10 years, with relative performance vs SPY over 1y/5y/10y. Same scoring engine as the short-term ranker but optimized for longer investment horizons and secular trends.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: Momentum-Ranker-Long Today's data shows LINC (Lincoln National) dominating the long-term momentum rankings with a rank-1 score of 95.5, driven by extraordinary returns: 1,275.74% over 10 years, 475.69% over 5 years, and 140.64% over the past year. The stock passes all four ratio thresholds and sits above all 200-day moving averages, generating a "strong_buy" signal with clean momentum and confirmed volatility expansion. In stark contrast, CF (CF Industries) ranks second with a 61.43% one-year return but shows momentum deterioration—its 6-month return (48.51%) actually declined from its 3-month return (58.08%), indicating the rally may be losing steam, and it significantly underperformed SPY over 10 years (+30.58% vs SPY's much larger gains). LINC's correlation pattern suggests materials-sector strength (XLB at 0.326), while CF shows weaker long-term positioning despite near-term price strength, highlighting a divergence between cyclical momentum and secular trend sustainability.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
